comment_text.sn19.17:1.4: The bad monastics are tormented by the requisites that are the signs of their monastic commitment, offered to them out of faith by the lay community.

root_text.sn19.17:1.1: “Idhāhaṁ, āvuso, gijjhakūṭā pabbatā orohanto addasaṁ bhikkhuṁ vehāsaṁ gacchantaṁ.
root_text.sn19.17:1.2: Tassa saṅghāṭipi ādittā sampajjalitā sajotibhūtā, pattopi āditto sampajjalito sajotibhūto, kāyabandhanampi ādittaṁ sampajjalitaṁ sajotibhūtaṁ, kāyopi āditto sampajjalito sajotibhūto.
root_text.sn19.17:1.4: eso, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kassapassa sammāsambuddhassa pāvacane pāpabhikkhu ahosi …pe….

translation_text.sn19.17:1.1: “Just now, reverend, as I was descending from Vulture’s Peak Mountain I saw a monk flying through the air.
translation_text.sn19.17:1.2: His outer robe, bowl, belt, and body were burning, blazing, and glowing
translation_text.sn19.17:1.4: “That monk used to be a bad monk in the time of Buddha Kassapa’s dispensation. …”
